compiled_state.title: Create plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh with state write/read/cleanup/resume functions
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## TDD Requirement
T1 (bd67-c045) writes the failing tests first. This task implements sprint-pause-state.sh to make all T1 tests pass (GREEN).

## Implementation Notes
Create plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh as an executable bash script. Mirror the merge-helpers.sh pattern exactly (same TTL, same mtime check, same JSON-via-python3, same atomic write via .tmp + mv).

### State File Path
/tmp/sprint-pause-state-<sanitized-epic-id>.json
(sanitize: replace / and whitespace with -)
Override path with SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR env var for testing.

### JSON Schema
{
  "epic_id": "<epic-id>",
  "stories": [],
  "story_answers": {},
  "in_progress_marker": false,
  "created_at": <epoch-float>
}

### Functions / CLI Subcommands
- init <epic-id>: create state file if not fresh; no-op if fresh
- write <epic-id> <field> <value>: update a field in the JSON (use python3 for atomic write via .tmp + mv)
- read <epic-id>: print state JSON to stdout; exit 1 if absent
- is-fresh <epic-id>: exit 0 if file exists and mtime < 240 min; exit 1 otherwise
- cleanup <epic-id>: remove state file; exit 0 always
- stale-cleanup: find /tmp -maxdepth 1 -name 'sprint-pause-state-*.json' -mmin +240 -delete 2>/dev/null; also support SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR override
- resume-context <epic-id>: read state file, output first story ID from stories[] whose key is absent from story_answers{}; exit 1 if no unanswered stories

### Functions for SIGURG trap
_spause_file_path <epic-id>: echo state file path
_spause_sigurg_handler: called on SIGURG/Ctrl-C; sets in_progress_marker=false via python3 atomic write; does NOT remove state file (preserve for --resume); exits 0
(The SIGURG handler leaves state file intact so sprint can offer --resume on re-invocation)

### Flag Gate
Read planning.external_dependency_block_enabled via read-config.sh at script start.
When flag is false (or absent/error): all subcommands are no-ops (exit 0, no file created).
Use SPRINT_PAUSE_ENABLED env var override for testing (SPRINT_PAUSE_ENABLED=false → no-op).

### Security
Never write confirmation tokens or secrets to the state file.
in_progress_marker is a boolean. story_answers values are story IDs, not tokens.

### Script Structure
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -uo pipefail
# [shebang + header]
# [PLUGIN_ROOT resolution via BASH_SOURCE + readlink]
# [flag gate check]
# [helper functions]
# [CLI dispatch: case "$1" in init|write|read|is-fresh|cleanup|stale-cleanup|resume-context)]

### PLUGIN_ROOT Resolution
Use standard pattern:
  _SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
  _PLUGIN_ROOT="$(cd "$_SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

### Error Handling
All python3 state I/O uses || true (best-effort, fail-open). set -e must not propagate from partial writes.

## Acceptance Criteria
Universal:
- [ ] bash tests/scripts/test-sprint-pause-state.sh passes all 11 tests (exit 0, FAILED: 0)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-pause-state.sh 2>&1 | grep "FAILED: 0"
- [ ] ruff check passes (no Python touched)
  Verify: echo "N/A - no Python files modified by this task"

Task-specific:
- [ ] plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh
- [ ] init subcommand creates state file with all 5 required fields
  Verify: SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR=$(mktemp -d) && bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh init test-epic && python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR/sprint-pause-state-test-epic.json')); assert all(k in d for k in ['epic_id','stories','story_answers','in_progress_marker','created_at']); print('ok')"
- [ ] cleanup subcommand removes state file (exit 0 even when file absent)
  Verify: SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR=$(mktemp -d) && bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh cleanup test-epic && echo "ok"
- [ ] stale-cleanup removes files older than TTL but preserves fresh files
  Verify: SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR=$(mktemp -d) && bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh init test-fresh && touch -t 202001010000.00 "$SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR/sprint-pause-state-old-epic.json" && bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh stale-cleanup && test -f "$SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR/sprint-pause-state-test-fresh.json" && ! test -f "$SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR/sprint-pause-state-old-epic.json" && echo "ok"
- [ ] SIGURG handler sets in_progress_marker=false and preserves state file
  Verify: SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR=$(mktemp -d) && bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh init test-epic && source $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh && _spause_sigurg_handler && python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR/sprint-pause-state-test-epic.json')); assert d['in_progress_marker']==False; print('ok')"
- [ ] Flag-off noop: SPRINT_PAUSE_ENABLED=false causes init to exit 0 with no file created
  Verify: SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR=$(mktemp -d) && SPRINT_PAUSE_ENABLED=false bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh init test-epic && ! test -f "$SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR/sprint-pause-state-test-epic.json" && echo "ok"
- [ ] check-shim-refs.sh passes (no direct plugin path references)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/hooks/check-shim-refs.sh 2>&1 | grep -v "sprint-pause-state.sh" | grep -q "OK\|no violations" || true
- [ ] check-portability.sh passes
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/hooks/check-portability.sh 2>&1 | grep -v "FAIL" | head -1

comments.body:
AC amendment: Document python3 unavailability fallback for is-fresh.

When python3 is unavailable or the mtime check fails, is-fresh must exit 1 (treat-as-stale, fail-open) to prevent hung sessions where a missing interpreter causes the sprint to incorrectly believe it can resume.

Add to acceptance criteria:
- [ ] is-fresh subcommand exits 1 (stale) when python3 is unavailable or mtime check throws exception
  Verify: SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR=$(mktemp -d) && bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh init test-epic && (PATH=/nonexistent bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh is-fresh test-epic; exit_code=$?; test $exit_code -ne 0 || echo 'ok') || echo 'fallback ok'
comments.author: Test
